Lockouts implemented by employers during labor disputes must comply with established bargaining obligations and cannot be used to undermine union rights.
Collective bargaining agreements establish binding terms regarding wages, hours, and working conditions.
Equal pay provisions mandate that employees performing substantially equal work receive comparable compensation regardless of protected characteristics.

Downtown revitalization programs often include tax incentives and regulatory flexibility to encourage private investment.
Urban renewal programs authorize municipalities to acquire, clear, and redevelop blighted or deteriorated areas.
Overlay districts impose additional regulatory requirements on underlying zoning classifications for historic or environmental protection.
Developers must obtain all requisite permits before commencing construction activities on any proposed urban development project.
